$revision_note: length-bias fix: distractors expanded for q1,q3,q5

questions.objective_ref: Define what a post-mortem is and state its purpose in an AI system context.
Describe the four sections of a post-mortem and explain what belongs in each section.
Distinguish between proximate cause and root cause and place each in the correct post-mortem section.
Propose a specific, named governance control as the prevention answer for a given failure.
Recognise the difference between a blame-assigning and a no-blame post-mortem and explain why no-blame produces more useful governance improvements.
Describe the four sections of a post-mortem and explain what belongs in each section.
Apply the four-section framework to a Week 10 case study and produce a structured 200-word post-mortem.

lo_coverage: LO1 — Define what a post-mortem is and state its purpose (q1)
LO2 — Describe the four sections and what belongs in each (q2, q6)
LO3 — Apply the four-section framework to a case study (q7)
LO4 — Distinguish proximate cause from root cause (q3)
LO5 — Propose a specific, named governance control (q4)
LO6 — Recognise no-blame vs. blame-assigning framing (q5)
